{"id":236,"date":"2026-06-25T13:47:11","date_gmt":"2026-06-25T13:47:11","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/beesayatv.test\/?post_type=trail&p=236"},"modified":"2026-07-14T10:01:40","modified_gmt":"2026-07-14T10:01:40","slug":"ylaya-camp-8","status":"publish","type":"trail","link":"http:\/\/beesayatv.test\/trails\/ylaya-camp-8\/","title":{"rendered":"Camp 8 Backtrails | Minglanilla Part 3 | 2026 GPX"},"content":{"rendered":"\n

Camp 8 Backtrails is a short out-and-back hike through the farmland trails of Ylaya in Camp 8, Minglanilla. The route follows rolling countryside with a gradual climb to its highest point before returning along the same trail. The moderate elevation gain and straightforward navigation make it suitable for hikers looking for a short mountain walk without demanding ascents.<\/p>\n\n\n

Trail Snapshot<\/h3>
EASY<\/div>View others \u2192<\/a><\/div>
\n

\ud83d\udca7 Water<\/span>At least 2 liters<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83c\udf33 Shade<\/span>Moderate<\/strong><\/div>\n
\ud83d\udc63 Surface<\/span>80% trail, 20% road<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\uddfa\ufe0f GPX<\/span>Supporter Access<\/a><\/strong><\/div> <\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Access<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The documented hike began from a roadside jump-off in Ylaya, Camp 8, which was reached via a concrete road. Motorcycle parking was available along the roadside, and no parking fee was observed during the hike. Private vehicles could access the starting point, while public transportation was limited. Habal-habal services were available.<\/p>\n\n\n

Why this trail is rated Easy<\/h3>
\u2713 Easy-to-follow trail<\/div>
\u2713 Navigation is straightforward<\/div>
\u2713 No technical climbing required<\/div>
\u2713 Route commitment is low<\/div>
\u2713 Short hiking distance<\/div><\/div>

On this trail:<\/span> farmland<\/a> \u2022<\/span> valley<\/a> \u2022<\/span> viewpoint<\/a><\/p><\/div><\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Route Description<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The route begins with a gradual climb through farmland and rolling hills, with the elevation gain spread steadily across the trail rather than concentrated in steep sections. The hike continues to the highest point before returning along the same countryside paths.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

The return is generally easier, although a few rolling sections remain along the route. The trail is non-technical and follows a straightforward path throughout.<\/p>\n\n\n\n\n\n\n

Trail Conditions<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

Conditions during the documented hike were sunny and dry. The trail was dry at the time but can become muddy after rain. The opening section was shaded, while most of the route was exposed. No stores were encountered along the trail.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Mobile signal was strong near the trailhead before becoming weak or disappearing farther along the route. At least 2 liters of water was recommended. Few insects and low foot traffic were observed during the hike.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Additional Notes<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

This route forms a small segment of the larger Minglanilla Trail Run 2026 route.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

The trail passes through farmland and is not considered isolated.<\/p>\n\n\n\n
